etymology_and_history.root_language: Language**: Old French "denier" from Latin "denegare" (to deny completely)
etymology_and_history.original_meaning: Meaning**: To deny completely; refusing acknowledgment and rejecting reality supported by acceptance awareness
etymology_and_history.semantic_evolution: Evolution**: Complete refusal → refusing acknowledgment → contemporary reality rejection and truth avoidance that benefits from integration growth
etymology_and_history.cultural_shifts: Shifts**: Complete refusal → refusing acknowledgment → reality rejection → acceptance understanding

contemporary_usage.primary_definition: Definition**: Experiencing reality rejection and truth avoidance; showing refusal acknowledgment and protective disbelief calling for integration support and acceptance development
contemporary_usage.register: Serious negative; indicates avoidance calling for integration and acceptance support

- **Frequency Trend**: Very high; critical for d
